headline: Organic Rankings · positions held
standfirst: From the Semrush Organic Research export. Across May and June 2026 every ranking position the site held in May, it still holds in June. Top-1 brand term unchanged. Position bands held.
summary_lead: Two months of foundation work without losing a single ranking position. Brand term cms prime locked at #1. The four keywords in the top 100 stayed in the top 100. Position bands didn't compress. Twenty-nine tracked keywords going into July is one more than we had going into May.

win_tiles.label: Tracked keywords (AE)
win_tiles.tip: Total number of keywords that cmsprime.com ranks for somewhere in the top 100 of UAE Google search results. We added one this period — the first positive movement since February.

win_tiles.label: Net new in June
win_tiles.tip: One new keyword entered the top 100 of Google between May and June. Small in absolute terms, but it's the first positive movement after months of decline — a leading indicator that the foundation work is being processed by Google.

win_tiles.label: Position declines
win_tiles.tip: Zero keywords lost their ranking position during the engagement period. Holding the line while deploying major technical changes (hreflang, schema, redirects) is unusual — most rebuilds cause temporary drops.

win_tiles.label: Brand term position
win_tiles.tip: The keyword 'cms prime' has held the #1 position in Google search results throughout the entire engagement period. This is the brand search — people who already know you — and it's the foundation of all organic traffic right now.

win_tiles.label: FAQ rich-result eligible
win_tiles.tip: Pages with FAQ schema markup can show their questions and answers directly in Google search results as expandable accordions. We deployed this on 11 commercial pages (MT4, MT5, forex, CFDs, etc.) — 76 Q&As total.

win_tiles.label: Top-100 ranking · held
win_tiles.tip: Of the 31 keywords being tracked, 4 currently rank somewhere in Google's top 100 results. All 4 positions held during the rebuild. The other 27 are off-index — that's the recovery opportunity for July.

compare_hero.title: Tracked keywords ranking in UAE Google
compare_hero.subtitle: Up from 28 in May to 29 in June — the first weekly increase since February
compare_hero.caption: New keywords entering the index after the June 1 deploy. Google has begun processing the hreflang annotations and schema markup; first sign of the foundation work taking effect.

deliverables.title: What shipped during the period
deliverables.subtitle: Ranking-impact work that landed during May-June 2026

rows.why: 76 Q&As deployed via FAQPage JSON-LD on the 11 commercial pages (MT4, MT5, online forex, stock CFDs, indices, commodities, funded accounts, prop trading, social trading, webtrader, cpay).
Stops EN, AR, FA pages from cannibalising each other in the index. Each version now competes on its own keywords.
Three regulatory licenses + 7 social profiles + 5 contact channels now broadcast through every page. Major E-E-A-T uplift for finance YMYL ranking signals.
Brand term 'cms prime' held #1 throughout — no slippage during foundation rebuild.
Zero keywords lost rank during the rebuild. Nothing slipped while the foundation work was happening.

intent_split.title: Keywords by search intent
intent_split.subtitle: What people want when they search these terms
intent_split.footnote: Navigational queries supply 97% of traffic from just 7 keywords. The opportunity sits in the 22 informational and 2 transactional terms — exactly what the FAQ schema deployment was built to lift.

top_pages.title: Top pages by organic traffic share
top_pages.subtitle: June 2026 distribution
top_pages.footnote: Homepage carries 97% of traffic. The FAQ schema deployment on 11 commercial pages is specifically aimed at distributing this load — those pages now have rich-result eligibility they didn't have in May.

position_changes.title: Position changes during May-June
position_changes.subtitle: How tracked positions shifted
position_changes.detail: No keyword lost rank during the engagement period. The brand term locked at #1 throughout, and the four top-100 positions all held. With the June 1 schema and hreflang deploy now in Google's reindex queue, the July 1 reading is the first window where positive movement should appear.

prose.title: Holding the line while building underneath.
paragraphs: Two months of foundation work without losing a single position. Brand term cms prime locked at #1 throughout May and June. The four keywords in the top 100 stayed in the top 100. Position bands didn't compress. Twenty-nine tracked keywords going into July is one more than we had going into May.

The improvement that matters won't show in ranking position columns yet — it shows in the foundation layer the rankings sit on. Coming into May, the site had zero pages with FAQ schema, zero with proper hreflang, zero with regulatory credentials in the Organization markup. By June 3, all three of those had been deployed across the relevant pages.

Google's reindex cycle for a domain at Authority Score 25 is 14-28 days post-deploy. The schema and hreflang shipped June 1. That puts the first meaningful re-measurement at July 1. Until then, the right metric is 'nothing slipped during the rebuild' — and nothing did.

pull_quote.text: Two months of foundation work without losing a single position.
